test/app.purpose: Integration test root for app marketplace integrations — wires all services from raw package constructors against a real pgtestdb Postgres DB and validates app lifecycle, marketplace listings, and invoice sync without importing app/common or Wire provider sets. Its custominvoicing/ and stripe/ children own the per-provider invoice-sync and lifecycle suites.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface + testEnv struct
patterns.description: testenv.go defines a TestEnv interface (Adapter(), App(), Close()) with a compile-time assertion and a concrete testEnv. NewTestEnv wires every service via direct package constructors.
patterns.example: type TestEnv interface { Adapter() app.Adapter; App() app.Service; Close() error }; var _ TestEnv = (*testEnv)(nil)

patterns.name: migrate.Up() before service construction
patterns.description: Each environment provisions Postgres via testutils.InitPostgresDB(t), runs migrator.Up() with migrate.OMMigrationsConfig, and defers migrator.CloseOrLogError() immediately.
patterns.example: migrator, _ := migrate.New(migrate.MigrateOptions{ConnectionString: driver.URL, Migrations: migrate.OMMigrationsConfig}); if err := migrator.Up(); err != nil { t.Fatalf(...) }; defer migrator.CloseOrLogError()

patterns.name: InitBillingService shared helper
patterns.description: Billing construction is extracted into InitBillingService(t, ctx, InitBillingServiceInput) (validated via Input.Validate()) so app_test.go and stripe/invoice_test.go reuse the same wiring chain; it builds the entitlement registry, meter mockadapter, taxcode, lockr, and billingservice.New with ForegroundAdvancementStrategy.
patterns.example: billingService, err := InitBillingService(t, ctx, InitBillingServiceInput{DBClient: entClient, CustomerService: customerService, AppService: appService})

patterns.name: eventbus.NewMock(t) for the publisher
patterns.description: All environments use eventbus.NewMock(t) instead of a real Kafka publisher — the only sanctioned publisher mock here.

patterns.name: closerFunc closes entClient AND driver.EntDriver
patterns.description: The testEnv closerFunc must close both entClient and driver.EntDriver, joining errors with errors.Join; missing either leaks a DB connection.
patterns.example: closerFunc := func() error { var errs error; if err = entClient.Close(); err != nil { errs = errors.Join(errs, err) }; if err = driver.EntDriver.Close(); err != nil { errs = errors.Join(errs, err) }; return errs }

patterns.name: setupNamespace(t) per test method
patterns.description: AppHandlerTestSuite.setupNamespace(t) assigns a fresh ULID namespace at the top of every test method for full isolation; not-found assertions use models.IsGenericNotFoundError.
patterns.example: func (s *AppHandlerTestSuite) TestGetMarketplaceListing(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T) { s.setupNamespace(t); service := s.Env.App(); ... }

key_file_guides.role: Constructs the full app service graph; defines TestEnv, testEnv, NewTestEnv, and the InitBillingService helper + InitBillingServiceInput.Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never import app/common; always defer migrator.CloseOrLogError(); closerFunc must close both entClient and driver.EntDriver; validate InitBillingServiceInput before constructing.
Call setupNamespace(t) first in each method; use models.IsGenericNotFoundError for not-found, not string matching.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: creates TestEnv, defers Close(), runs AppHandlerTestSuite sub-tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Defer ctx cancel; report env.Close() errors via t.Errorf (not require.NoError) so cleanup completes.

key_file_guides.role: Child: async invoice-sync protocol tests (draft→issuing→payment) embedding billingtest.BaseSuite.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Drive the state machine through BillingService/CustomInvoicingService only; defer MockStreamingConnector.Reset().

key_file_guides.role: Child: Stripe install/CRUD + invoice-sync tests using mock Stripe clients (TestEnv interface and billingtest.BaseSuite architectures coexist).
key_file_guides.watch_for: defer stripeClient.Restore()/stripeAppClient.Restore(); stable-sort invoice lines before setting AddInvoiceLines expectations.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or Wire provider sets in testenv.go — wire from raw constructors (appadapter.New, appservice.New, etc.)
Sharing a namespace string across AppHandlerTestSuite methods — call setupNamespace(t) per method
Calling Stripe client methods directly instead of through app.Service or appstripe.Service
Forgetting defer stripeClient.Restore()/stripeAppClient.Restore() in stripe sub-tests — leftover expectations poison later tests
Adding invoice-line assertions without stable-sorting lines before setting mock expectations

decisions.decision: InitBillingService is a shared helper, not inline construction
decisions.rationale: app_test.go and stripe/invoice_test.go both need a billing.Service; extracting it avoids duplication and keeps testenv.go focused on app wiring.

decisions.decision: Two parallel test architectures: TestEnv interface vs billingtest.BaseSuite embedding
decisions.rationale: Simple marketplace tests need only App()/Adapter(); invoice-sync tests need the full billing suite machinery — both coexist.

decisions.decision: Raw package constructors only, no app/common imports
decisions.rationale: app/common imports all domain packages; importing it from tests creates cycles and forces every domain to compile for a single test.

test/entitlement.purpose: Structural parent for entitlement integration and regression test packages — owns the test/entitlement/regression sub-package that guards grant burn-down, expiry, voiding, and reset edge cases against real Postgres using raw package constructors and MockStreamingConnector. The directory itself holds only a CLAUDE.md.

patterns.name: No source files at parent level
patterns.description: All test code lives in sub-packages (regression/); never add .go files directly to test/entitlement.

patterns.name: setupDependencies for self-contained bootstrap
patterns.description: Each regression test file wires its own dependency graph via setupDependencies using raw package constructors plus MockStreamingConnector — never app/common.
patterns.example: deps := setupDependencies(t) // real Ent adapters + MockStreamingConnector

patterns.name: Controlled clock with defer reset
patterns.description: Time-based grant burn-down tests advance the global clock via clock.SetTime() and must defer clock.ResetTime() to avoid leaking state into parallel tests.

patterns.name: Scenario tests named after the bug they guard
patterns.description: Regression functions in scenario_test.go are named after the specific production bug/edge case for immediate root-cause traceability.

key_file_guides.role: Defines setupDependencies and createCustomerAndSubject helpers plus shared test wiring.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Pre-existing context.Background() uses are not a model — new code uses t.Context(). Never import app/common.

key_file_guides.role: Individual regression tests, one per production bug; each calls setupDependencies independently (fresh pgtestdb DB).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never reuse subject keys or feature keys across test functions.

anti_patterns: Adding .go source files directly in test/entitlement — all code belongs in regression/
Importing app/common or any Wire provider set — causes import cycles
Using context.Background() in new test code where t.Context() is available
Reusing subject or feature keys across tests without isolated setupDependencies calls
Skipping defer clock.ResetTime() — leaves global clock state dirty for parallel tests

decisions.decision: Regression tests isolated here rather than co-located with openmeter/entitlement/
decisions.rationale: Keeps the regression harness separate from production code so test-only imports (pgtestdb, MockStreamingConnector) don't pollute the production package graph.
ClickHouse is unavailable in standard CI; the mock gives deterministic usage responses without external dependencies.
